summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an expired proxy-auth JWT still accesses a protected route and renews into a fresh JWT when File Browser is configured with proxy auth and a custom logout page.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that the same expired JWT reads a protected file and renews successfully when logoutPage is custom, while the same token is rejected once logoutPage is reset to /login.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the pinned repository, copied an in-package regression test into the HTTP package, and executed go test inside the container. The test confirmed protected-route access and token renewal with a custom logout page, then confirmed 401 rejection for both routes after resetting logoutPage to /login.
verification_method: The reproduction injects a focused Go test into the cloned repository and asserts four conditions in-process: protected raw-download succeeds with an expired JWT under custom logout, renew succeeds with the same expired JWT, protected raw-download fails with 401 under default logout, and renew fails with 401 under default logout.
evidence_produced_by_run: go test ./http -run TestExpiredProxyAuthTokenCustomLogoutBypass exited successfully inside the reproduction container.
The container printed the verification marker: [evohunt] VERIFIED: expired proxy-auth JWT bypassed protected-route expiry with custom logout and was rejected with default logout.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
